Great Northern passenger train No.
3. which is due to arrive from the
west tonight at 2 p. m, will make
an attempt to pass the flooded district
on the line between Boomers Ferry
and Troy.


The utmost vigilance will be exact
cd of the trainmen and the manage
ment has given orders that the speed
of the train must be reduced to al
most = creep In passing over the
dangerous section.


Laborers have been working like
Trojans ever since the tieup to get
the grade repaired sufficiently to
carry the tonnage OF the company
until the waters subside. After that
to safeguard themselves against an
other of these disastrous foods men
will be Immediately set to work fill
ing In the bottom. or sink as some
ca. :. with rock and making a foun-
dalton high enough and solid enough
to be above any more such floods and
to withstand the undermining action
OF the water, which during the pres
